evidence_excerpt: Continues to do well; ambulatory–independent; ongoing serial high‑dose methotrexate (cycle #15) with no documented progression.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing fatigue, transient left facial/head numbness episodes, and a persistent productive cough despite antibiotics; denies fevers, sweats, weight loss.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Admitted for cycle #15 high-dose methotrexate; will return for treatment in 2 months.
Discharge condition: Ambulatory - Independent; mental status clear and coherent.
Denies other headache or focal neuro symptoms; balance is intact (relative to initial headaches/seizures at diagnosis).
Complains of swelling and transient numbness of the left face/head triggered by emotional situations; refers to these as 'seizures'.
Cough productive of yellow sputum has continued despite a course of amoxicillin and another antibiotic.
Has had laser treatment of his left eye since his last admission.
Hyponatremia noted; instructed to hold desmopressin for one day then resume.
Oncologic history includes intra-ocular methotrexate treatments.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact dates are not provided; cycle #15 implies a long-term time frame post-diagnosis. MRI result is pending, so radiographic disease status is not documented here. Functional improvement is inferred from current independent ambulation compared to initial presentation with headaches/seizures. The nature of transient facial episodes is unclear (patient-labeled ‘seizures’ without tonic-clonic activity). Hyponatremia/transaminitis appear mild; categorized as deterioration due to objective abnormalities but not necessarily major complications.
